info.title: Twilio Knowledge API
info.description: APIs for managing knowledge bases and knowledge content for AI-powered applications.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all knowledge sources for a specific knowledge base.  Knowledge sources represent
unstructured data sources such as documents,  websites, or text content that can be used for context and information
retrieval.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page index. This value is simply for client state.

knowledge.name: Company FAQ Knowledge Base
knowledge.description: Frequently asked questions and answers for customer support

post.description:
Create a new knowledge source from various data sources such as web content,  files, or raw text. The
knowledge source will be processed and indexed to enable semantic search and retrieval.

## Best practices

To maximize the effectiveness of Knowledge, consider the following best practices:

**Assess and optimize content:** Regularly evaluate your existing Knowledge sources for accuracy, relevance, and coverage.
Identify any gaps or outdated information that could hinder the Assistant's performance.

**Simplify and structure content:** Ensure that the content is clear and concise. Use headings, bullet points, and metadata to make information straightforward
to navigate for both the AI Assistant and your users.

**Prioritize high-impact content:** Focus on updating and
maintaining content that is frequently accessed or critical to customer interactions. Consider using analytics to
determine which Knowledge entries are most valuable.

example.name: Product Documentation
example.description: Comprehensive product documentation and user guides

value.name: Product Documentation
value.description: Comprehensive product documentation and user guides

fileSource.summary: File-based knowledge source with presigned upload URL

post.description:
Perform semantic search across knowledge sources within a knowledge base to find the most relevant content
chunks based on a natural language query.  Returns ranked chunks with similarity scores, allowing you to retrieve
contextually relevant information for AI applications, chatbots, or  information retrieval systems. You can filter
results by specific knowledge sources.

example.query: How do I reset my password?

chunks.content: To reset your password, go to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'. Enter your email address and follow the instructions in the reset email.
If you're having trouble with password reset, contact support at help@example.com or call 1-800-HELP.

get.description:
Fetch detailed information about a specific knowledge source by its ID.  This returns the complete knowledge
source object including  processing status, source details, and configuration information.

example.name: Company FAQ Knowledge Base
example.description: Frequently asked questions and answers for customer support

patch.description:
Partially update mutable fields of an existing knowledge source such as name, description, tags, or source-specific
configuration. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. Some changes (e.g., KnowledgeSourceTypes)
may trigger asynchronous reprocessing of the underlying content. Fields omitted from the request remain unchanged.
Immutable fields (id, type, status, url, createdAt, updatedAt) cannot be modified directly.

## Refresh ##

To request reprocessing without changing fields, pass the query parameter `refresh=true`. When `refresh=true` is provided,
the server will re-queue processing for this knowledge resource (transitioning the persisted status to `QUEUED`) and
return 202 Accepted. This query parameter is idempotent while the resource is already QUEUED or PROCESSING.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: When true, re-queues processing for this knowledge resource. Idempotent while the resource is already QUEUED or PROCESSING.

example.name: Updated FAQ Knowledge Base
example.description: Updated frequently asked questions and answers for customer support

202.description: Knowledge update accepted and is being processed.

delete.description: Permanently delete knowledge source and all its associated data, including  processed chunks and embeddings. This action cannot be undone. The knowledge  resource will no longer be available for search or retrieval operations.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all processed content chunks from a specific knowledge source.  Chunks are smaller segments of content that have been extracted and processed from the original  knowledge source. Each chunk contains content text and associated metadata that can be used  for semantic search and retrieval operations.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: How many resources to return in each list page. The default is 50, and the maximum is 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: The page token. This is provided by the API.

chunks.content: To reset your password, go to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'. Enter your email address and follow the instructions in the reset email.

get.description: Get a list of knowledge bases for the Twilio account.

post.description: Create a new Knowledge Base for the Twilio account. Accounts can have multiple knowledge bases. Each knowledge base can contain multiple knowledge resources such as documents, websites, or text content that can be used for context and information retrieval.

message.example: Knowledge Base creation request accepted for processing.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a specific Knowledge Base by its unique ID.

patch.description: Partially update a Knowledge Base. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated.

displayName.description: Provides a unique and addressable name to be assigned to this Knowledge Base. This name is assigned by the developer and can be used in addition to the ID. It is intended to be human-readable and unique within the account.

description.description: A human readable description of this resource, up to 128 characters.
description.example: Product documentation knowledge base for customer support

message.example: Knowledge Base update request accepted for processing.

delete.description: Delete a Knowledge Base and all associated knowledge resources. This action cannot be undone.

message.example: Knowledge Base deletion request accepted for processing.

parameters.name: operationId
parameters.description: The operation ID returned from a write endpoint.

get.description: Retrieve the status and result of an asynchronous operation.

key.description:
The key of the list property contains the actual data items.
This enables programmatic iteration over paginated results.

KnowledgeId.description: The unique identifier of knowledge source.

KnowledgeCore.description:
Core properties for knowledge source that can be set at creation time. Regularly update and organize your
Knowledge sources to ensure it contains the most accurate and relevant information. This includes adding new entries,
updating existing ones, and removing outdated content.

name.description: The name of the knowledge source.

description.description: A detailed description of the knowledge source and when to use it.  This helps provide context about the content and its intended purpose.

example.name: Company FAQ Knowledge Base
example.description: Frequently asked questions and answers for customer support

name.description: The name of the knowledge source.

description.description: A detailed description of the knowledge source and when to use it.  This helps provide context about the content and its intended purpose.

status.description: The status of processing the knowledge source ('SCHEDULED', 'QUEUED', 'PROCESSING', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED').

createdAt.description: The date and time in GMT when the Knowledge was created specified in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

updatedAt.description: The date and time in GMT when the Knowledge was last updated specified in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format.

KnowledgeChunk.description: Represents a processed content chunk extracted from knowledge source.  Chunks are smaller segments of content that have been parsed and indexed  for semantic search operations, containing the original text content.

example.content: To reset your password, go to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'.

createdAt.description:
The date and time in GMT when the Chunk was created specified in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
format.

KnowledgeSearch.description:
Request payload for performing semantic search across knowledge sources within the knowledge base..  Allows
querying with natural language text and filtering by specific knowledge  sources to find the most relevant content
chunks.

example.query: How do I reset my password?

query.description: The query to search the knowledge source.

top.description: The top K results to return.

knowledgeIds.description: The list of knowledge IDs to search.

createdAt.description:
The date and time in GMT when the Chunk was created specified in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
format.

score.description: The score associated with the chunk.

KnowledgeSourceTypes.description:
Details specific to the knowledge source type. Each knowledge source type has  its own set of configuration
parameters and source specific properties.

WebSourceDetails.description: Configuration details for web based knowledge sources

url.description: The URL to crawl for web content

crawlDepth.description: The maximum depth to crawl from the source URL

crawlPeriod.description: Frequency of re-crawling the website for updated content

TextSourceDetails.description: Configuration details for raw text knowledge sources

content.description: The raw text content to be processed
content.example: This is the raw text content that will be processed as knowledge.

example.content: This is the raw text content that will be processed as knowledge.

TwilioError.description: A generic error object communicating client and server side API errors.

message.description: A human readable message describing the error.

more_info.description: A URL to a [Twilio error directory](https://www.twilio.com/docs/api/errors) page with more information about the error code.

status.description: The HTTP status code for the error.

example.message: The address specified in the request is not a valid phone number.

key.description:
The key of the list property contains the actual data items.
This enables programmatic iteration over paginated results.

displayName.description:
Provides a unique and addressable name to be assigned to this Knowledge Base. This name is assigned
by the developer and can be used in addition to the ID. It is intended to be human-readable and unique within
the account.

description.description: A human readable description of this resource, up to 128 characters.
description.example: Product documentation knowledge base for customer support

displayName.description:
Provides a unique and addressable name to be assigned to this Knowledge Base. This name is assigned
by the developer and can be used in addition to the ID. It is intended to be human-readable and unique within
the account.

description.description: A human readable description of this resource, up to 128 characters.
description.example: Product documentation knowledge base for customer support

id.description: The unique identifier for the Knowledge Base

status.description: The provisioning status of the Knowledge Base

createdAt.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the Knowledge Base was created.

updatedAt.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the Knowledge Base was last updated.

version.description: The current version number of the Knowledge Base. Incremented on each successful mutable update.

knowledgeBases.description: List of Knowledge Bases associated with the Twilio account.

SupportedFileMimeType.description:
Supported MIME types for knowledge file imports. Maximum file size for any file is 16MB (16 * 1024 * 1024 bytes). Extensions → MIME:
  .csv → text/csv
  .md → text/markdown
  .mdx → text/mdx
  .pdf → application/pdf
  .tsv → text/tab-separated-values
  .txt → text/plain

FileSourceDetails.description:
Configuration details for file based knowledge sources. Supported file formats (extension → MIME type):
  .csv → text/csv
  .md → text/markdown
  .mdx → text/mdx
  .pdf → application/pdf
  .tsv → text/tab-separated-values
  .txt → text/plain
Maximum file size: 16MB (16 * 1024 * 1024 bytes).

fileName.description: Name of the file to be uploaded

fileSize.description: Expected size of the file in bytes

importUrl.description:
Presigned S3 URL for file upload (when status is SCHEDULED) or the permanent S3 location after upload completes.
Use PUT method to upload the file to this URL when status is SCHEDULED.

uploadExpiration.description: Expiration time of the presigned upload URL in ISO 8601 format (only present when status is SCHEDULED)

operationId.description: The unique identifier for this operation.

status.description: The current status of the operation.

completedAt.description: When the operation completed or failed.

type.description: A URI reference identifying the problem type.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

resultUrl.description: URL to fetch the resulting resource.

OperationResult.description: Operation-specific output. Present when status is COMPLETED.

id.description: The identifier of the created or affected resource.

KbId.name: kbId
KbId.description: A unique Knowledge Base ID using Twilio Type ID (TTID) format

PageSize.name: pageSize
PageSize.description: The maximum number of items to return per page, maximum of 1000.

PageToken.name: pageToken
PageToken.description: The token for the page of results to retrieve.

KnowledgeIdPath.name: knowledgeId
KnowledgeIdPath.description: A unique Knowledge resource ID using Twilio Type ID (TTID) format

ControlPlanePageSize.name: pageSize
ControlPlanePageSize.description: The maximum number of items to return per page, maximum of 100.

OrderBy.name: orderBy
OrderBy.description: Either 'ASC' or 'DESC' to sort results ascending or descending respectively.

IfMatchHeader.name: If-Match
IfMatchHeader.description:
Allows for optimistic concurrency control by making the request conditional.
Server will only act if the resource's current Entity Tag (ETag) matches
the one provided, preventing accidental overwrites.

example.message: Failed to complete request due to a bad request

TooManyRequests.description: This error indicates that you have sent too many requests to the API. You should retry according to the `Retry-After` response header.

example.message: Too many requests. Please retry according to the Retry-After response header.

Retry-After.description: The amount of time in seconds that the customer should wait before issuing a new request towards the API.

example.message: Failed to complete request due to lack of authentication credentials

ETag.description:
Entity tag that acts as a unique identifier for a specific version of the resource.
Send updates with If-Match conditional headers for optimistic concurrency control.

OperationRetryAfter.description: Suggested number of seconds to wait before checking status again.
